## ase-2026-research-track

- URL: https://conf.researchr.org/track/ase-2026/ase-2026-research-track
- Adapter: researchr
- Status: ok
- Changed: yes
- Hash: 04f202e1b9ea7c659cac5c86b6e9619b85bd24439d2e5b9a290712742607fd99
- Last changed: 2026-05-04T05:53:38.718Z

Candidate deadline-like dates:

- 2026-01-01, confidence 0.65: Starting January 1, 2026, ACM will fully transition to Open Access. All ACM publications, including those from ACM-sponsored conferences, will be 100% Open Access. Authors will have two primary options for publishing Open Access articles with ACM: the ACM Open institutional model or by paying Article Processing Charges (APCs). With over 1,800 institutions already part of ACM Open, the majority of ACM-sponsored conference papers will not require APCs from authors or conferences (currently, around 70-75%).

Candidate deadline-like dates:

- 2023-04-20, confidence 0.65: Note #4: Submissions must follow the “ ACM Policy on Authorship ” released April 20, 2023, which contains policy regarding the use of Generative AI tools and technologies, such as ChatGPT. Please also check the ACM FAQ which describes in what situations generative AI tools can be used (with or without acknowledgement).
- 2023-04-20, confidence 0.65: A: Submissions must follow the “ ACM Policy on Authorship ” released April 20, 2023, which contains policy regarding the use of Generative AI tools and technologies, such as ChatGPT. Please also check the ACM FAQ which describes in what situations generative AI tools can be used (with or without acknowledgment).
- 2025-09-04 abstract, confidence 0.65: Paper registration: September 4, 2025 (to register a paper, paper title, abstract, author list, and some additional metadata are required; title and abstract must contain sufficient information for effective bidding; registrations containing empty or generic title and abstract may be dropped)
- 2025-09-11 full_paper, confidence 0.65: Full paper submission: September 11, 2025
- 2025-12-22 notification, confidence 0.65: Initial notification: December 22, 2025
- 2026-01-01, confidence 0.65: Starting January 1, 2026, ACM will fully transition to Open Access. All ACM publications, including those from ACM-sponsored conferences, will be 100% Open Access. Authors will have two primary options for publishing Open Access articles with ACM: the ACM Open institutional model or by paying Article Processing Charges (APCs). With over 1,800 institutions already part of ACM Open, the majority of ACM-sponsored conference papers will not require APCs from authors or conferences (currently, around 70-75%).
- 2026-02-24 full_paper, confidence 0.65: Revised manuscript submissions (major revisions only): February 24, 2026
- 2026-03-24 notification, confidence 0.65: Final notification for major revisions: March 24, 2026
- 2026-04-23 camera_ready, confidence 0.65: Camera ready: April 23, 2026
